authors: Kon, Liu, Qiu, Fan, He, Lin, Zhang, Park, Elengikal, Kang, Chen, Chowdhury, Lee, Wang
title: IaC-Eval: A Code Generation Benchmark for Cloud Infrastructure-as-Code Programs
key_findings: GPT-4 achieves 19.36% pass@1 vs 86.6% on EvalPlus; foundational benchmark establishing IaC generation gap
notes: Foundational benchmark paper. Published at NeurIPS. GitHub: autoiac-project/iac-eval

title: TerraFormer: Automated Infrastructure-as-Code with LLMs Fine-Tuned via Policy-Guided Verifier Feedback
model_types: Sonnet 3.7, DeepSeek-R1, GPT-4.1, CodeLlama, custom fine-tuned
tool_use_external_verifier: yes - formal verification tools (tflint, terraform validate, Checkov, tfsec)
key_findings: +15.94% on IaC-Eval over base LLM; outperforms 50x larger models on TF-Gen/TF-Mutn; achieves top security compliance
notes: Strongest paper in field. Neuro-symbolic approach. Accepted ICSE 2026 SEIP. ONLY paper with explicit security compliance scoring

title: Multi-Agent Code-Orchestrated Generation for Reliable Infrastructure-as-Code (MACOG)
agent_architecture: Architect + Provider Harmonizer + Engineer + Reviewer + Security Prover + Cost Planner + DevOps + Memory Curator
key_findings: GPT-5 improves from 54.90 (RAG) to 74.02 with MACOG; includes Security Prover agent and OPA policy enforcement
notes: Multi-agent architecture with dedicated Security Prover. Uses OPA for policy. arXiv only (not peer-reviewed)

title: IaC Generation with LLMs: An Error Taxonomy and A Study on Configuration Knowledge Injection
key_findings: Baseline 27.1% overall success; knowledge injection raises technical validation to 75.3% and overall to 62.6%; identifies Correctness-Congruence Gap
notes: Key contribution: two-dimensional error taxonomy (validation stage x failure pattern). Identifies 'Correctness-Congruence Gap' where LLMs become good coders but poor architects

title: A Survey of using Large Language Models for Generating Infrastructure as Code
key_findings: Only existing survey of LLM+IaC; covers early landscape but limited in scope (few papers); already outdated given 2025-2026 explosion
notes: Published at ICON 2023 (small NLP conference). Very shallow. No systematic methodology. No quality assessment. Useful only as baseline comparison for our review
